text: Anticoagulants are generally contraindicated in people with active bleeding conditions, gastrointestinal ulcers, low platelets, severe liver disease, or uncontrolled hypertension, as these increase the risk of serious bleeding. Warfarin should not be given to people with heparin-induced thrombocytopenia until platelet count has improved. It is usually avoided in people with protein C or protein S deficiency due to risk of skin necrosis.
